decisions.title: Prioritize Core Algorithm Performance vs Feature Polish
decisions.context: Engineering capacity split between core algorithm improvements (ENG-9000: large-file skipping, channel processing) and UI polish (Family Overview, Data Selector, File Viewer). Need to align with 'Nail the Core' pillar vs user-facing improvements.

arguments.for: Core algorithm work directly impacts Decisions Captured by enabling faster analysis on larger datasets. Large-file skipping could unlock 30%+ more decisions per customer.
arguments.against: UI polish (Family Overview, File Viewer) removes friction in decision workflow. Poor UX blocks adoption of existing capabilities, limiting decisions captured today.

decisions.title: Productize E2 Chart Types in Live Mode or Defer
decisions.context: ENG-5352 has been open since 2024. Live Mode with full chart types would enable real-time decision-making (aligns with Sift Edge vision), but continued deferral suggests it's not critical path.

arguments.for: Live Mode with all chart types is table stakes for Sift Edge positioning. Real-time decisions are highest-value use case. Kills 2-year-old tech debt.
arguments.against: No customer escalations on this feature. E2 cache improvements (ENG-6614, ENG-8416) may be higher ROI for decision velocity. Resources better spent on Python Rules integration.

decisions.title: Accelerate Alerting Infrastructure Buildout
decisions.context: Multiple alert configuration tickets (ENG-8975, ENG-8976, ENG-8979, ENG-8980) suggest infrastructure maturity push. Critical for 'Build the Moat' via operational excellence, but unclear if this is customer-facing or internal operations.

arguments.for: Production reliability directly impacts customer trust. Disk fill alerts (ENG-8976) prevent data loss incidents that would destroy Decisions Captured metric. Required for enterprise accounts.
arguments.against: These are operational hygiene, not customer value. No evidence customers are blocked. Team capacity better spent on Canvas or Python Rules that directly drive North Star.

decisions.title: Resolve Family Overview Architecture: Analysis Tab Ownership
decisions.context: Three separate tickets on Family Overview (ENG-8951 polish, ENG-9012 rules tab, ENG-7852 stats preview, ENG-8952 config polish) suggest unclear product definition. 'Embed Deeper' pillar requires coherent family-based workflows.
decisions.blockedBy: Unclear whether Family Overview is primarily a configuration surface (ENG-8952) or analysis surface (ENG-9012). Design spec needed before FE work continues.

arguments.for: Family-based workflows are key to 'Embed Deeper' - customers need single pane for config + analysis. Consolidating now prevents rework when Python Rules launches.
arguments.against: Over-engineering the Family concept before we validate Python Rules adoption. Ship minimal Family UI, iterate based on beta feedback from Q2 Python Rules launch.

decisions.title: HDF5 Native Imports: Beta or Hold for Python Rules
decisions.context: ENG-8415 (Native HDF5 imports) open. HDF5 is common in aerospace/automotive. Could be 'Embed Deeper' play, but unclear if this should launch with Python Rules or independently.

arguments.for: HDF5 support unblocks key verticals (aerospace uses HDF5 heavily). Could drive 15-20% increase in Decisions Captured in those accounts by removing export friction.
arguments.against: Format support is table stakes, not differentiator. Python Rules lets users transform HDF5 themselves. Better to nail Python Rules than add format #47.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy 1:1 week (11 scheduled). Engineering sync meetings consuming 40% of week. Only 8 focus hours across 5 days.
alerts: Zero decision-making time scheduled this week despite 5 open strategic decisions
Back-to-back meetings Thursday 9am-5pm blocks any deep work on Python Rules roadmap
22 meetings with only 2 outcomes-focused (Sprint Planning, Roadmap Review). Consider async standups.
